codeReview.summary: The stdio proxy implementation covers child spawning, bidirectional line forwarding, EOF propagation, and signal forwarding, but two blocking correctness problems remain: wrapper diagnostics share the proxied stderr stream, and the post-exit 3-second drain timeout can drop trailing stdout/stderr bytes under backpressure.

issues.description: `tracing_subscriber::fmt().with_writer(std::io::stderr)` sends wrapper diagnostics to the same stderr stream that is supposed to transparently proxy the child's stderr. Because `wrapper/src/proxy.rs` emits `info!`/`error!` records on normal lifecycle and error paths, enabling `RUST_LOG` or hitting an error injects wrapper-generated bytes into the proxied stderr output, so stderr is no longer a direct byte-for-byte passthrough.

After `child.wait()` completes, `run_proxy_with_io()` only waits up to 3 seconds for the stdout/stderr forwarding tasks and then returns even if they are still draining. With a slow downstream reader or enough buffered child output, the wrapper will terminate and drop the remaining bytes, which breaks the feature's transparent byte-for-byte forwarding guarantee.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-worker skill still tells wrapper workers to run `cd wrapper && cargo clippy` directly, but this Debian environment does not have clippy on PATH and this worker had to deviate to `cargo check`. The skill should include the documented workaround or an explicit fallback so workers do not keep rediscovering the same environment limitation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md:39 requires direct `cargo clippy`; .factory/library/environment.md:35 documents that `cargo clippy` is unavailable by default; handoffs/2026-03-31T06-27-53-081Z__wrapper-stdio-proxy__fae250b8-2d48-4ff0-b71b-513f557b662a.json:137-142 records the deviation to `cargo check`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared service commands expose wrapper build/test entry points but no standard wrapper clippy/lint command, even though wrapper verification steps depend on clippy and the environment requires a non-trivial workaround. That leaves workers to invent ad-hoc wrapper lint commands instead of reusing a documented service command.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml:10-12 defines `build-wrapper` and `test-wrapper` only; .factory/library/environment.md:35 describes the extracted-`rust-clippy` workaround needed in this environment.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the mission docs, transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit 71ad97cb15249064a3817d620e7b9defd4f37178. The feature implements the core stdio proxy flow, but it is not yet transparent enough to pass review because wrapper logs contaminate the proxied stderr stream and the hardcoded 3-second drain timeout can drop trailing child output.
